title: Referential Integrity and Overflow Tables
text: Referential Integrity and Overflow Tables The relationship between a base table and its overflow table is managed by Uniface. When describing relationships and foreign key rules to the database connector, Uniface inserts the appropriate relationships and rules for overflow tables. As a rule, database connectors do not need to distinguish between base tables and overflow tables. Connectors that do, can determine the nature of the relationship by looking at RelationshipType->IsOverflow . Related Links Referential Integrity Limitations Overflow Tables DBMSs may impose upper limits to the number of fields in a table, the length of a field, and the length of a record. Some DBMSs do not support variable-length fields with a high maximum length. To overcome these limitations, Uniface provides an overflow mechanism, in which data that is represented as a single modeled entity is automatically split into two tables—the base table and an overflow table. RelationshipType Describes the relationship between two tables. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
